info.title: MSSP Portal
info.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage and maintain multiple customer instances of Tenable products. Endpoints in the Tenable MSSP Portal API enables customers to view and manage your MSSP customer accounts. 

For more information about the Tenable MSSP Portal, see the [Tenable MSSP Portal User Guide](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/Welcome.htm).

tags.name: Accounts
tags.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage and maintain multiple customer instances of Tenable products. Account endpoints in the Tenable MSSP Portal API enables customers to view and manage your MSSP customer accounts. 

For more information about the Tenable MSSP Portal, see the [Tenable MSSP Portal User Guide](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/Welcome.htm).

tags.name: Account Groups
tags.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage and maintain multiple customer instances of Tenable products. The account group endpoints in the Tenable MSSP Portal API enable users to create, list, update, and delete account groups. Account groups enable you to group similar customer accounts and facilitates the search for specific accounts. 

For more information about account groups in the Tenable MSSP Portal, see [Create an Account Group](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/CreateAccountGroup.htm) in the Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) User Guide.

tags.name: Child Containers
tags.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage and maintain multiple customer instances of Tenable products. Child Containers endpoints in the Tenable MSSP Portal API enables customers to view and manage your child accounts. 

For more information about the Tenable MSSP Portal, see the [Tenable MSSP Portal User Guide](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/Welcome.htm).

tags.name: Dashboards
tags.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage and maintain multiple customer instances of Tenable products. Dashboard endpoints in the Tenable MSSP Portal API enables customers to retrieve details for various dashboard widgets. 

For more information about the Tenable MSSP Portal, see the [Tenable MSSP Portal User Guide](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/Welcome.htm).

tags.name: Domains
tags.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage and maintain multiple customer instances of Tenable products. Domains endpoints in the Tenable MSSP Portal API enables customers to add domains, list domains, get domain details, update domains, and send activation codes to add domains. 

For more information about the Tenable MSSP Portal, see the [Tenable MSSP Portal User Guide](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/Welcome.htm).

tags.name: Filters
tags.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage and maintain multiple customer instances of Tenable products. Filters endpoints in the Tenable MSSP Portal API enables customers to list the filtering capabilities for the other MSSP endpoints that support filtering. 

For more information about the Tenable MSSP Portal, see the [Tenable MSSP Portal User Guide](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/Welcome.htm).

tags.name: License
tags.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage and maintain multiple customer instances of Tenable products. License endpoints in the Tenable MSSP Portal API enables customers to retrieve license details for the account associated with the user's API credentials. 

For more information about the Tenable MSSP Portal, see the [Tenable MSSP Portal User Guide](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/Welcome.htm).

tags.name: Logos
tags.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage the logos of their customer's instances. By default, the Tenable logo appears in the header of your customer's instances. The logo endpoints enables customers to replace the Tenable logo with a logo appropriate to a customer's business context. Use these endpoints to add, assign, and delete logos. 

For background information about logos in the Tenable MSSP Portal, see [Logos](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/Logos.htm) in Tenable MSSP Portal User Guide.

tags.name: Partners
tags.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage their partner information. Partner endpoints in the Tenable MSSP Portal API enables customers to retrieve partner, distributor, and license information. 

For more information about the Tenable MSSP Portal, see the [Tenable MSSP Portal User Guide](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/Welcome.htm).

tags.name: Resource Links
tags.description:
The Tenable Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) Portal API provides a secure and accessible way for MSSP administrators to manage and maintain multiple customer instances of Tenable products. Resource Links endpoints in the Tenable MSSP Portal API enables customers to add resource links, bulk add resource links, update resource links, and list resource links. 

For more information about the Tenable MSSP Portal, see the [Tenable MSSP Portal User Guide](https://docs.tenable.com/managed-security-service-provider/Content/Welcome.htm).

get.description:
Returns a list of child accounts in the Tenable MSSP Portal. A child account contains information about a customer instance of a Tenable product, including product license information and user-defined notes that can include valuable internal tracking or customer contact information.
Requires the Basic [16] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of an account group. This parameter can be used to retrieve only child accounts in the specified account group.
parameters.name: group

parameters.description:
A filter condition in the format `field:operator:value`. For example, `f=container_name:match:tenable`. 

**Note:** To retrieve a list of filters to use with this endpoint, see the  [GET /filters/mssp/accounts](ref:io-mssp-filters-account-list) endpoint. 

See the list below for additional examples of possible filter combinations:  
* uuid:neq:afe4fa83-8430-4f7b-a4dd-c25bcfd21317  
* licensed_assets:gte:222  
* license_utilization:lt:100  
* license_expiration_date:gt:90  
* licensed_apps:eq:was,lumin  
* region:eq:US
parameters.name: f

parameters.description: If multiple `f` parameters are present, specifies whether Tenable MSSP applies `AND` or `OR` to conditions. Supported values are `and` and `or`. If you omit this parameter when using multiple `f` parameters, Tenable applies `AND` by default.
parameters.name: ft

parameters.description: Wildcard filter text. Wildcard search is a mechanism where multiple fields of a record are filtered against one specific filter string. If any one of the wildcard\_fields' values matches against the filter string, then the record matches the wildcard filter. For a record to be returned, it must pass the wildcard filter (if there is one) AND the set of standard filters. For example, if `w=wild&f=field1:match:one&f=field2:match:two&ft=or`, the record would match if the value of any supported wildcard\_fields contained `wild`, AND either field1's value contained `one` or field2's value contained `two`.
parameters.name: w

parameters.description:
A comma-delimited subset of wildcard\_fields to search when applying the wildcard filter. For example, `field1,field2`. If `w` is provided, but `wf` is not, then all wildcard\_fields' values are searched against the wildcard filter text. 

**Note:** To retrieve a list of fields that can be used as wild cards, see the [GET /filters/mssp/accounts](ref:io-mssp-filters-account-list) endpoint.
parameters.name: wf

200.description: Returned if the Tenable MSSP Portal successfully retrieved the list of child accounts.

accounts.notes: Some notes about this child account
Some notes about this child account

post.summary: Generate auth keys for child container
post.description:
Generates authorization keys for the specified child container. An `access_key` and a `secret_key` is included in the response.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

child_container_uuid.description: The UUID of the child container you want to generate authorization keys for.

keys_validity_duration_seconds.description: The number of seconds that the authorization keys should remain valid. The maximum value is `3600`. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable MSSP uses the default value of `1800` seconds.

200.description: Returned if the Tenable MSSP Platform successfully generated authorization keys for the specified child container.

parent_container_uuid.description: The UUID of the parent container.

child_container_uuid.description: The UUID of the child container associated with the parent customer account.

child_container_site.description: The cloud region in which the child container is located.

keys_user_uuid.description: The UUID of the read-only MSSP child keys user.

access_key.description: The generated access key. It must be paired with the `secret_key`.

secret_key.description: The generated secret key. It must be paired with the `access_key`.

keys_expiration_epoch_seconds.description: The date and time in Unix format when the authorization keys expire.

remote.description: Indicates whether or not the parent and child containers are in the same cloud region. If `true`, the parent and child containers are located in different cloud regions. If `false`, the parent and child containers are located in the same cloud region.

_validity_info.description: The date and time in datetime format when the authorization keys expire.

400.description: Returned if your request specified invalid parameters or if your request was improperly formatted.

404.description: Returned if the Tenable MSSP Platform could not find the specified child container.

429.description: Returned if you attempt to send too many requests in a specific period of time. For more information, see [Rate Limiting](doc:rate-limiting).

response.value:
429 Too Many Requests

get.description:
Returns the data for the specified dashboard widget.
Requires the Basic [16] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.name: widget
parameters.description:
The dashboard widget you want to retrieve data for. You can retrieve data for two dashboard widgets:
  • customer_licensing_data—This widget provides a quick list of customer accounts and their basic licensing snapshot.
  • scan_results—This widget provides a list of customer accounts and some high level scan metadata for each one.

post.description:
Adds a logo to the Tenable MSSP Portal. After you add a logo to the Tenable MSSP Portal, use the [PUT /mssp/accounts/logos](ref:io-mssp-logos-assign) endpoint to assign the logo to a customer account.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

logo.description: The logo to upload. The logo must be in PNG format and no larger than 246x52 pixels.

name.description: An identifiable, user-defined name for the logo.

parameters.name: logo_uuid
parameters.description: The UUID of the logo to update.

logo.description: The logo to upload. The logo must be in PNG format and no larger than 246x52 pixels.

name.description: An identifiable, user-defined name for the logo.

200.description: Returned if Tenable MSSP Portal successfully updated the logo.

400.description: Returned if your request specifies invalid query parameter values.

value.error: request should be multipart and include a single 'name' part

404.description: Returned if the Tenable MSSP Portal cannot find the specified logo.

value.error: logo with uuid 8d20527a-c651-457e-9f9f-c26503045011 not found on container d6eacae7-7e1e-457e-9e4f-e86716db564e

delete.description:
Deletes the specified logo in the Tenable MSSP Portal.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of the logo to delete.

200.description: Returned if Tenable MSSP Portal successfully deletes the specified logo.

put.description:
Assigns a logo to one or more customer accounts in the Tenable MSSP Portal.
Requires the Basic [16] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

200.description: Returned if Tenable MSSP Portal successfully assigns the specified logo to the specified account(s).

400.description: Returned if your request specifies an invalid `logo_uuid` or `account_uuids` query parameter value.

post.description:
Adds or updates resource links for multiple customer accounts.
Requires the Basic [16] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parent_container_uuid.description: The UUID of the parent container for the child containers you want to bulk add resource links for.

container_uuid_list.description: An array of child containers that you want to bulk add resource links for.

resource_links.description: An array of resource links to bulk add to the child containers.

200.description: Returned if the Tenable MSSP Platform successfully bulk added the resource links for the specified customer accounts.

status.description: The status of the bulk add operation.

400.description: Returned if your request specified invalid parameters or if your request was improperly formatted.

500.description: Returned if an internal error occurred in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

value.message: An internal server error occurred. Please wait a moment and try your request again.

post.description:
Adds or updates resource links for the specified customer account.
Requires the Basic [16] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.name: account_uuid
parameters.description: The UUID of the customer account you want to add resource links for.

schema.description: An array of resource links to add to the specified account.

items.description: An array of resource links to add to the specified account.

200.description: Returned if the Tenable MSSP Platform successfully added the resource links for the specified customer account.

container_uuid.description: The UUID of the container where the resource links were added.

code.description: The status code associated with the operation results.

message.description: The status message associated with the operation results.

message.message: Successfully modified resource links for given container UUID

400.description: Returned if your request specified invalid parameters or if your request was improperly formatted.

500.description: Returned if an internal error occurred in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

value.message: An internal server error occurred. Please wait a moment and try your request again.

get.description:
Returns the resource links for the specified account.
Requires the Basic [16] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.name: account_uuid
parameters.description: The UUID of the customer account you want to retrieve resource links for.

200.description: Returned if the Tenable MSSP Platform successfully retrieved the resource links for the specified customer account.

container_uuid.description: The UUID of the container associated with the resource links.

resource_links.description: An array of resource link objects.

404.description: Returned if the Tenable MSSP Platform could not find the specified customer account.

429.description: Returned if you attempt to send too many requests in a specific period of time. For more information, see [Rate Limiting](doc:rate-limiting).

response.value:

accounts.description: An array of customer account objects.

history.description: An array of child container history objects.

domains_list.description: An array of customer account objects.

accounts.description: An array of customer account objects.

uuid.description: The UUID of the customer account in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

container_name.description: The name of the customer account in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

licensed_assets.description: The number of assets currently counted against the customer's Tenable license.

licensed_assets_limit.description: The number of assets which the customer is licensed to scan.

domains.description: A list of the customer domains.

distributor.description: A list of the partner's distributors.

products.description: A list of the partner's available products.

contact_email.description: The email address used for contacting the partner.

countries.description: An array of strings defining the list of countries where the partner is allowed to operate.

name.description: The name of the product that the partner is licensed to distribute.

sku.description: The SKU of the Tenable product that the partner is licensed for.

minQuantity.description: The minimum number of assets that can be requested when creating a quote with the [Create quote](ref:mssp-accounts-quote) endpoint.

maxQuantity.description: The maximum number of assets that can be requested when creating a quote with the [Create quote](ref:mssp-accounts-quote) endpoint.

success.description: Indicates whether or not Tenable MSSP successfully created the new evaluation account.

message.example: successfully created new eval container and sfdc evaluation
message.description: A short message about the creation of the evaluation account.

name.description: The name of the evaluation account. The name is the specified email domain followed by a dash and random integers. For example `example.com-830759035`.

uuid.description: The UUID of the new evaluation account.

success.description: Indicates whether or not Tenable MSSP successfully created the quote.

message.description: A short message about the creation of the quote.

name.description: The name associated with the quote. This corresponds with the `admin_username`.

Licensed-Apps.description:
An array of strings defining the list of licensed Tenable applications For example, `["vm", "was", "lumin"]`. Valid strings include the following:
  • ad—Tenable Identity Exposure
  • asm—Tenable Attack Surface Management
  • lumin—Tenable Lumin
  • one—Tenable One
  • vm—Tenable Vulnerability Management
  • was—Tenable Web App Scanning

sku.description: The product SKU for the Tenable application you're requesting. You can use the [Get partner details](ref:mssp-partner-details) endpoint to retrieve the list of SKUs that can be requested from the specified partner.

assets.description: The number of assets you want to license for the requested Tenable product. This number must fall between the `minQuantity` and `maxQuantity` range for the Tenable application's SKU from the partner. You can retrieve these values from the [Get partner details](ref:mssp-partner-details) endpoint.

expiration_date.description: The date when the account expires. The expiration date must be specified in YYYY-MM-DD format and it must be at least 90 days in the future.

name.description: The name of the resource link.

url.description: The URL of the resource link.

uuid.description: The UUID of the customer account in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

container_name.description: The name of the customer account in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

lms_customer_id.description: The customer's licensing management system customer ID.

custom_name.description: A custom name in addition to the container name to help you more easily identify the customer / container.

region.description: The Tenable AWS cloud region that contains the customer account. Customer account regions correspond to the regions used for [cloud sensors](https://docs.tenable.com/vulnerability-management/Content/Platform/Settings/Sensors/CloudSensors.htm). To avoid latency issues, Tenable recommends that scans in the customer instance use scanners in the same region as the customer account.

licensed_assets.description: The number of assets currently counted against the customer's license.

licensed_assets_limit.description: The number of assets which the customer is licensed to scan.

notes.description: User-defined text; for example, internal tracking or customer contact information.

logo_uuid.description: The UUID of the logo assigned to the customer account in the Tenable MSSP Portal. This attribute is only present if a custom logo is assigned to the account.

domains.description: A list of the customer domains.

created.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time on which the container was created; for example, `2021-01-16T12:00:00Z`.

modified.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time on which the container was last modified; for example, `2021-01-16T12:00:00Z`.

parent_uuid.description: The UUID of the parent account.

account_uuids.description: An array of child account UUIDs that you want to link with the parent account.

uuid.description: The UUID of the customer account in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

child_container_uuid.description: The UUID of the child container associated with the parent customer account.

user_name.description: The user name associated with the change.

old_value.description: The old value of the item that was changed.

new_value.description: The new value of the item that was changed.

created.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time on which the change was made; for example, `2021-01-16T12:00:00Z`.

status.description: The status of the domain. For example, `ACTIVE`, ACTIVATION_PENDING`, or `DISABLED`.

type.description: The type of domain. For example, `PRIMARY` or `SECONDARY`.

date_added.description: The Unix timestamp when the domain was added.

container_uuid.description: The UUID of the child container in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

domains.description: An array of domains associated with the container.

message.description: A short status message about the operation.

filters.description: A list of filters available for the record type.

name.description: The field name to be used in request query strings when applying the filter.

operators.description: Strings which represent the comparison operations which can be used for the filter.

readable_regex.description: Provides a human-readable "hint" which describes what the filter string should look like.

type.description: The type of UI control which represents the filter.

regex.description: A regex which can be used by a user interface to validate input.

max_length.description: The max length of the filter.

group_name.description: Always set to `NULL`. Legacy attribute associated with deprecated functionality.

wildcard_fields.description: Array of strings which represent each field which supports "wildcard" search. Wildcard search is a mechanism where multiple fields of a record are filtered against one specific filter string. If any one of the supported fields' values matches against the filter string, then the record matches the wildcard filter. Note that for a record to be returned, it must pass the wildcard filter (if there is one) AND the set of standard filters.

container_uuid.description: The UUID of the container to add the new domain to.

domain_name.description: The domain you want to add to the container.

activation_code.description: The required activation code to add the domain to the specified container.

container_uuid.description: The UUID of the container to add the new domain to.

domain_name.description: The domain you want to add to the container.

host_name.description: The host name associated with the domain name. Typically this is the first part of the email address of the user you want to send the verification to. For example, for user@example.com, `user` would be the host name.

is_resend.description: Indicates whether or not to resend the verification code.

email.description: The MSSP partner's email address. A valid email address must be in the format `name@domain`.

country.description: The primary contact's country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format. Valid country codes are `AU`, `BR`, `CA`, `EU`, `IN`, `JP`, `SG`, `UK`, and `US`.

customer_name.description: The name of the customer account.

default_contact_email.description: The default email address used for contacting the owner of the evaluation account. The domain name of the email address must match the MSSP partner's domain name.

admin_username.description: A unique username for the evaluation account. It must be in email format, ex. `customer@partner.com`, and the domain name of the email address must match the MSSP partner's domain name.

country.description: The primary contact's country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format. Valid country codes are `AU`, `BR`, `CA`, `EU`, `IN`, `JP`, `SG`, `UK`, and `US`.

admin_username.description: A unique username for the evaluation account. It must be in email format, ex. `customer@partner.com`, and the domain name of the email address must match the MSSP partner's domain name.

customer_name.description: The name of the customer account.

licensed_apps.description: An array of licensed Tenable applications and the amount of assets per application.

region.description: The primary contact's country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format. Valid country codes are `AU`, `BR`, `CA`, `EU`, `IN`, `JP`, `SG`, `UK`, and `US`.

requester.description: The quote requester's first and last name.

licensee.description: The quote licensee's first and last name.

id.description: The ID of the partner in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

name.description: The name of the partner in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

lms_customer_id.description: The partner's licensing management system customer ID.

id.description: The ID of the distributor in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

name.description: The name of the distributor in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

lms_customer_id.description: The distributor's licensing management system customer ID.

email.description: The default email address used for contacting the customer. The domain name of the email address must match the MSSP partner's domain name.

street.description: The street address for the customer's address.

city.description: The city for the customer's address.

state.description: The state for the customer's address. For example `MD`.

zip.description: The zip code for the customer's address.

country.description: The customer's country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format. For example, `US`. The country should match one of the countries from the chosen distributor. You can use the [Get partner details](ref:mssp-partner-details) endpoint to retrieve a list of a distributor's countries.

firstname.description: The first name of the customer that requested the quote.

lastname.description: The last name of the customer that requested the quote.

name.description:
The name of the account group.

**Note:** Account group names can only contain letters, numbers, and whitespace.

accountUuids.description: An array of account UUIDs to add to the account group.

accountGroupUuid.description: The UUID of the account group.

parentContainerUuid.description: The UUID of the account group's parent container.

name.description: The name of the account group.

lastModified.description: A Unix timestamp indicating the date and time when the account group was last modified. For example, `1710256733`.

count.description: The number of accounts contained in the account group.

accountUuids.description: An array of account UUIDs that belong to the account group.

container_uuid.description: The UUID of the container that contains the logo.

name.description: The user-defined name of the logo.

logo_uuid.description: The UUID of a logo in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

account_uuids.description: An array of customer account UUIDs in the Tenable MSSP Portal.

error.description: A brief message about the missing or invalid parameter value(s).
A brief message about the object that could not be found.

statusCode.description: The HTTP status code of the error.

message.description: A brief message about the cause of the error.
